title.en: Reading the Optical Wireless Standard: Scope, Vocabulary, and Conventions
title.zh: 读懂光无线通信标准：范围、术语与书写约定

blocks.en: Before you can read any IEEE standard fluently, you need three things: a clear picture of what it covers, a shared vocabulary, and a set of rules for how the document writes things down. IEEE Std 802.15.13-2023 — the standard for Multi-Gigabit-per-Second Optical Wireless Communications (OWC), meaning data sent over light rather than radio waves — front-loads exactly this in its opening clauses. This lesson is a guided tour of Clause 1 (Overview), Clause 3 (Definitions, acronyms, and abbreviations), and Clause 4 (Conventions). Master these and the dense technical clauses that follow stop looking like a foreign language.
blocks.zh: 要想流畅地阅读任何一份 IEEE 标准，你需要三样东西：清楚标准覆盖的范围、一套共享的术语，以及文档书写信息的规则。IEEE Std 802.15.13-2023——这份关于「多吉比特每秒光无线通信」（OWC，即用光而非无线电波传输数据）的标准——在开篇几个条款里正好先交代了这些内容。本课将带你逐条游览第 1 章（概述）、第 3 章（定义、缩略语与缩写）和第 4 章（书写约定）。掌握它们之后，后面那些密集的技术条款就不再像天书了。

blocks.en: Clause 1.1 (Scope) tells you the boundaries of the standard. IEEE 802.15.13 defines a physical layer (PHY — the part that actually puts bits onto the medium) and a medium access control (MAC) sublayer (the part that decides who transmits when), using light at wavelengths from 10 000 nm down to 190 nm in optically transparent media. It can deliver data rates up to 2.192 Gb/s over unrestricted line-of-sight ranges up to 200 m, supporting both point-to-point and point-to-multipoint links, and adapts to changing channel conditions. Clause 1.2 (Purpose) explains the why: enabling secure, high-performance, low-latency, very robust links for industrial and similar applications — and crucially, links that do not interfere with existing radio-frequency (RF) systems, since light and radio occupy entirely different bands.
blocks.zh: 第 1.1 节（范围）告诉你标准的边界。IEEE 802.15.13 定义了一个物理层（PHY，即真正把比特送上传输媒介的那一层）和一个介质访问控制（MAC）子层（决定谁在何时发送的那一层），在光学透明媒介中使用波长从 10 000 nm 一直到 190 nm 的光。它能在不受遮挡的视距范围（最远 200 m）内提供高达 2.192 Gb/s 的数据率，支持点对点与点对多点链路，并能适应变化的信道条件。第 1.2 节（目的）解释了为什么：为工业及类似应用提供安全、高性能、低时延、极其稳健的链路——而且关键在于，这些链路不会干扰现有的射频（RF）系统，因为光和无线电占用的是完全不同的频段。

blocks.en: Clause 1.3 (Word usage) is small but mission-critical. In IEEE prose, ordinary English words have precise meanings: shall = a mandatory requirement (is required to); should = a recommendation (is recommended that); may = a permitted option (is permitted to); can = a statement of possibility or capability (is able to). The words must and will are deprecated for requirements. When you read 'a device shall set the field to zero,' that is law; 'should' is advice. Reading these words loosely is the single most common way to misunderstand a standard.
blocks.zh: 第 1.3 节（用词约定）篇幅虽小却至关重要。在 IEEE 文风中，普通英文词有精确含义：shall（应）＝强制性要求（必须做到）；should（宜）＝推荐做法（建议这样做）；may（可）＝允许的选项（被准许这样做）；can（能）＝对可能性或能力的陈述（有能力做到）。在表述要求时，must 与 will 已被弃用。当你读到「设备 shall 把该字段置零」时，那是铁律；而「should」只是建议。把这些词读得含糊，是误解标准最常见的方式。

blocks.en: Clause 3.1 (Definitions) hands you the standard's working vocabulary. The central object is the optical wireless personal area network (OWPAN): a group of at least one coordinator plus one or more members, all sharing connectivity. A coordinator is the device that organizes and actively maintains the OWPAN; a member is associated with the OWPAN but is not the coordinator. A device is simply any entity implementing a conformant MAC and PHY interface to the wireless medium, and association is the service that establishes a device's membership in the OWPAN. Definitions also cover the optical frontend (OFE) — the actual interface to the light channel, one transmitter plus one receiver — and the fronthaul, the connection between a coordinator and its optical frontends.
blocks.zh: 第 3.1 节（定义）交给你这份标准的工作词汇。核心对象是光无线个域网（OWPAN）：由至少一个协调器加一个或多个成员组成、彼此互联的一组设备。协调器（coordinator）是负责组织并主动维护 OWPAN 的设备；成员（member）已加入 OWPAN 但不是协调器。设备（device）就是任何实现了符合标准的 MAC 与 PHY、并接入无线媒介的实体；而关联（association）是用来确立设备在 OWPAN 中成员身份的服务。定义还涵盖光前端（OFE）——即接入光信道的实际接口，由一个发射器加一个接收器构成——以及前传（fronthaul），即协调器与其各光前端之间的连接。

blocks.en: Several definitions describe how data and topology are organized. A frame is the format of bits from a MAC sublayer entity that are transmitted together in time; the payload is the contents being carried inside it; and a fragment is one contiguous piece of a MAC protocol data unit (MPDU). For reach beyond a direct link, a relay device is a member that forwards data between the coordinator and another member; that forwarded member is the relayed device, and the indirect path between them is a relay link. Finally, in scheduled medium access, each superframe (a recurring time structure) is tagged with a consecutive superframe number so devices can keep their timing aligned.
blocks.zh: 还有几个定义描述了数据与拓扑的组织方式。帧（frame）是 MAC 子层实体产生、在时间上一起发送的比特的格式；净荷（payload）是帧中承载的内容；分片（fragment）则是一个 MAC 协议数据单元（MPDU）中连续的一段。为了延伸到直连之外，中继设备（relay device）是一个在协调器与另一成员之间转发数据的成员；被转发的那个成员叫被中继设备（relayed device），两者之间的间接路径称为中继链路（relay link）。最后，在调度式介质访问中，每个超帧（superframe，一种循环的时间结构）都被打上连续递增的超帧号（superframe number），以便各设备保持时序对齐。

blocks.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 440 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"><rect x="180" y="15" width="90" height="34" rx="4"/><text x="225" y="37" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">Coordinator</text><rect x="30" y="120" width="80" height="34" rx="4"/><text x="70" y="142" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">Member</text><rect x="185" y="120" width="80" height="34" rx="4"/><text x="225" y="142" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">Relay dev.</text><rect x="340" y="120" width="90" height="34" rx="4"/><text x="385" y="138" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Relayed</text><text x="385" y="150" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">device</text><line x1="205" y1="49" x2="80" y2="120"/><line x1="225" y1="49" x2="225" y2="120"/><line x1="265" y1="137" x2="340" y2="137" stroke-dasharray="5 4"/><text x="300" y="112" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle">relay link</text></svg>

caption.en: An OWPAN: one coordinator and several members. Direct links are solid; a relay device extends reach to a relayed device over an indirect relay link (dashed).
caption.zh: 一个 OWPAN：一个协调器和若干成员。直连链路用实线表示；中继设备通过间接的中继链路（虚线）将覆盖延伸到被中继设备。

blocks.en: Clause 3.2 (Acronyms and abbreviations) is the decoder ring for the whole document. Layering terms include PHY (physical layer), MAC (medium access control), and their data units: MSDU and MPDU (MAC service / protocol data unit) and PSDU and PPDU (PHY service / protocol data unit). Service access points (SAPs) name the boundaries between layers — MD-SAP and PD-SAP for data, MLME-SAP and PLME-SAP for management — while the MLME (MAC layer management entity) and PIB (PAN information base, the device's store of configuration attributes) handle management state. Signal-processing terms include OFDM (orthogonal frequency division multiplexing), QAM (quadrature amplitude modulation), 2-PAM (binary pulse-amplitude modulation), MCS (modulation and coding scheme), MIMO (multiple-input multiple-output), LDPC (low-density parity-check code), FEC (forward error correction), and CRC / FCS (cyclic redundancy check / frame check sequence).
blocks.zh: 第 3.2 节（缩略语与缩写）是整篇文档的解码表。分层术语包括 PHY（物理层）、MAC（介质访问控制）及其数据单元：MSDU 与 MPDU（MAC 服务／协议数据单元）、PSDU 与 PPDU（PHY 服务／协议数据单元）。服务访问点（SAP）标示层与层之间的边界——数据用 MD-SAP 与 PD-SAP，管理用 MLME-SAP 与 PLME-SAP——而 MLME（MAC 层管理实体）和 PIB（个域网信息库，即设备保存配置属性的地方）负责管理状态。信号处理术语包括 OFDM（正交频分复用）、QAM（正交幅度调制）、2-PAM（二进制脉冲幅度调制）、MCS（调制与编码方案）、MIMO（多入多出）、LDPC（低密度奇偶校验码）、FEC（前向纠错），以及 CRC／FCS（循环冗余校验／帧校验序列）。

blocks.en: Clause 4 (Conventions) sets the typographic and formatting rules. Clause 4.1 (Names) is a quick parsing aid: constants and PIB attributes are written in italics with no spaces; constants carry the prefix 'a' (e.g., aMinFragmentSize); variable MAC attributes start with 'mac' (e.g., macOwpanId); and variable PHY attributes start with 'phy' (e.g., phyClockRate). Names of frames, elements, or fields are capitalized, italic, and may contain spaces — for example, the Association Request element. Once you internalize these prefixes, you can tell at a glance whether a name refers to a fixed constant, a tunable MAC knob, a PHY knob, or a piece of a frame.
blocks.zh: 第 4 章（书写约定）规定了排版与格式规则。第 4.1 节（命名）是快速识别的帮手：常量和 PIB 属性以斜体书写且不含空格；常量带前缀「a」（如 aMinFragmentSize）；可变的 MAC 属性以「mac」开头（如 macOwpanId）；可变的 PHY 属性以「phy」开头（如 phyClockRate）。帧、元素或字段的名称则首字母大写、用斜体，并且可以含空格——例如 Association Request 元素。一旦记住这些前缀，你就能一眼看出某个名称指的是固定常量、可调的 MAC 旋钮、PHY 旋钮，还是帧的某一部分。

blocks.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 460 130" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"><rect x="20" y="40" width="70" height="28"/><rect x="90" y="40" width="70" height="28"/><rect x="160" y="40" width="110" height="28"/><rect x="270" y="40" width="90" height="28"/><rect x="360" y="40" width="80" height="28"/><text x="55" y="33" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Bit 0–3</text><text x="125" y="33" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">4 Bits</text><text x="215" y="33" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">2 Octets</text><text x="315" y="33" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">variable</text><text x="400" y="33" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">5/7 Octets</text><text x="55" y="58" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Field 1</text><text x="125" y="58" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Field 2</text><text x="215" y="58" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Field 3</text><text x="315" y="58" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Field 4</text><text x="400" y="58" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Field 5</text><line x1="20" y1="95" x2="440" y2="95" stroke-width="1.5"/><polygon points="440,95 432,91 432,99" fill="currentColor" stroke="none"/><text x="230" y="112" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">order of processing (transmission) →</text></svg>

caption.en: Clause 4.2 / 4.3 in one strip: top line is each field's width (bits, octets, a slash-separated set like 5/7, or 'variable'); fixed-position fields can be named by first–last bit (Bit 0–3). Fields are processed left to right.
caption.zh: 一图看懂第 4.2／4.3 节：上行是每个字段的宽度（比特、八位组、用斜杠分隔的取值集合如 5/7，或「variable」可变）；位置固定的字段可用首位—末位标注（Bit 0–3）。字段按从左到右的顺序处理。

blocks.en: Clauses 4.2 and 4.3 govern how frames are drawn and ordered. Clause 4.2 (Frame and element representation) says frames and elements appear as small tables: the top line gives each field's width (a count of bits or octets, with 'Bits'/'Octets' spelled out; a slash-separated set like '5/7' for discrete choices; a dash for a range; or 'variable'), the middle line names the field, and an optional bottom line adds description. Where no variable-width field precedes it, a field can be located by its first and last bit (e.g., Bit 0–3). Clause 4.3 (Byte order and representation) defines processing order: bits, fields, and elements are transmitted and interpreted left to right as drawn. Multi-byte fields use big-endian order — the most significant byte is processed first and shown leftmost. In a bitmap, bit 0 is the least significant bit (LSB) of the first octet and bit 7 is the most significant bit (MSB); bit 8 is the LSB of the second octet, and so on.
blocks.zh: 第 4.2 与 4.3 节规定了帧如何绘制与排序。第 4.2 节（帧与元素表示）规定帧和元素以小表格形式出现：上行给出每个字段的宽度（比特或八位组的数量，并写明「Bits／Octets」；离散取值用斜杠分隔如「5/7」；范围用短横线；可变则写「variable」），中行给出字段名称，可选的下行补充描述。当一个字段之前没有可变宽度字段时，可用其首位与末位来定位（如 Bit 0–3）。第 4.3 节（字节顺序与表示）定义处理顺序：比特、字段和元素按图中从左到右的顺序发送和解释。多字节字段采用大端序——最高有效字节最先处理、显示在最左。在位图中，比特 0 是首个八位组的最低有效位（LSB），比特 7 是最高有效位（MSB）；比特 8 是第二个八位组的 LSB，依此类推。

blocks.en: Clauses 4.4 and 4.5 are easy to skip but save real debugging pain. Clause 4.4 (Numbers): values are written decimal (nnn), binary (0bnnn), or hexadecimal (0xnnn) — so 0b001 and 0x001 both mean one. Fields hold signed or unsigned integers, but unless a field's definition says otherwise, assume unsigned. MAC addresses use the IEEE 802 hex form: six hex octets joined by hyphens. Clause 4.5 (Reserved fields and values): a reserved field carries no meaning today and shall be set to all zeros on transmit and ignored on receive. A reserved value shall not be sent at all — and if you receive a frame whose field holds a reserved value, you shall drop the whole frame. Reserved space is how the standard leaves room to grow safely.
blocks.zh: 第 4.4 与 4.5 节容易被略过，却能省下实实在在的调试痛苦。第 4.4 节（数字）：数值以十进制（nnn）、二进制（0bnnn）或十六进制（0xnnn）书写——所以 0b001 和 0x001 都表示 1。字段存放有符号或无符号整数，但除非字段定义另有说明，否则按无符号处理。MAC 地址使用 IEEE 802 的十六进制形式：六个十六进制八位组用连字符相连。第 4.5 节（保留字段与取值）：保留字段在当前版本中没有含义，发送时 shall 全部置零，接收时 shall 忽略。保留取值则根本 shall 不被发送——而如果你收到某字段含保留取值的帧，你 shall 丢弃整个帧。保留空间正是标准为安全演进留出的余地。

summary.en: Coverage map — every clause taught here, by exact number
summary.zh: 覆盖地图——本课所讲每个条款及其精确编号

blocks.en: Clause 1 Overview: 1.1 Scope (PHY + MAC over light, 10 000–190 nm, up to 2.192 Gb/s, ranges to 200 m, point-to-point and point-to-multipoint); 1.2 Purpose (secure, robust, low-latency industrial links that do not interfere with RF); 1.3 Word usage (shall / should / may / can; must and will deprecated). Clause 3: 3.1 Definitions (association, coordinator, device, fragment, frame, fronthaul, MAC address, member, optical frontend (OFE), OWPAN, payload, relay device, relayed device, relay link, superframe number); 3.2 Acronyms and abbreviations (the full list — e.g., PHY, MAC, MPDU/MSDU, PPDU/PSDU, OFDM, QAM, 2-PAM, MCS, MIMO, LDPC, FEC, CRC, FCS, MLME, PIB, OFE, OWPAN, SAP). Clause 4 Conventions: 4.1 Names (italic no-space constants/attributes with 'a'/'mac'/'phy' prefixes; capitalized italic frame/element/field names); 4.2 Frame and element representation (table figures; width line, name line, optional description line; bits/octets, slash sets, ranges, 'variable'; first–last-bit notation); 4.3 Byte order and representation (left-to-right processing, big-endian multi-byte fields, bit-0-is-LSB bitmaps); 4.4 Numbers (decimal/0b/0x, default unsigned, IEEE 802 hyphenated MAC addresses); 4.5 Reserved fields and values (reserved fields set to zero / ignored; reserved values never sent, received frames carrying them dropped).
blocks.zh: 第 1 章 概述：1.1 范围（基于光的 PHY＋MAC，10 000–190 nm，最高 2.192 Gb/s，覆盖至 200 m，点对点与点对多点）；1.2 目的（安全、稳健、低时延且不干扰射频的工业链路）；1.3 用词约定（shall／should／may／can；must 与 will 已弃用）。第 3 章：3.1 定义（关联、协调器、设备、分片、帧、前传、MAC 地址、成员、光前端 OFE、OWPAN、净荷、中继设备、被中继设备、中继链路、超帧号）；3.2 缩略语与缩写（完整清单——如 PHY、MAC、MPDU/MSDU、PPDU/PSDU、OFDM、QAM、2-PAM、MCS、MIMO、LDPC、FEC、CRC、FCS、MLME、PIB、OFE、OWPAN、SAP）。第 4 章 书写约定：4.1 命名（斜体无空格的常量／属性，前缀 a／mac／phy；首字母大写斜体的帧／元素／字段名）；4.2 帧与元素表示（表格式图；宽度行、名称行、可选描述行；比特／八位组、斜杠取值集、范围、「variable」；首位—末位记法）；4.3 字节顺序与表示（从左到右处理、多字节字段大端序、比特 0 为 LSB 的位图）；4.4 数字（十进制／0b／0x、默认无符号、IEEE 802 连字符分隔的 MAC 地址）；4.5 保留字段与取值（保留字段置零／忽略；保留取值绝不发送，收到含保留取值的帧则丢弃）。

cites.note: Clause 1 (1.1 Scope, 1.2 Purpose, 1.3 Word usage) p.17; Clause 3 (3.1 Definitions p.18-19, 3.2 Acronyms p.19-21); Clause 4 (4.1 Names p.21, 4.2 Frame/element representation p.21-22, 4.3 Byte order p.22, 4.4 Numbers p.23, 4.5 Reserved fields and values p.23).
